DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF CLIFFORD COOK'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART. AS TO THE INTERROGATORIES, THE COURT WILL MODIFY THE QUESTIONS TO ONLY REQUIRE DISCLOSURE OF SUSPENSIONS PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO CHARTER SECTION A8.341 FOR ACTS WHICH WOULD CONSTITUTE A FELONY OR MISDEMEANOR INVOLVING MORAL TURPITUDE. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT MAY REDACT THE NAMES OF SAID PERSONS WHILE ASSIGNING A NUMERICAL IDENTIFIER. THE RESPONSES SHALL IDENTIFY THE ETHNICITY OF ANY OFFICERS. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW. MODIFICATION: TENTATIVE MODIFIED TO REQUIRE DISCLOSURE OF WHETHER ANY OFFICERS SUSPENDED WERE FORMALLY CHARGED BY COMPLAINT OR INDICTMENT WITH COMMITTING A PUBLIC OFFENSE. DEFENDANT SHALL SUBMIT DOCUMENTS FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW IN 30 DAYS. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.
APPEARANCES: John Houston Scott, Esq. of Scott Law Firm 561-9601 for pltf & opposing party; Margaret W. Baumgartner, Deputy City Atty 554-3859 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Court made a personal disclosure. No objections were made. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Failure to shift burden. No declaration from decision maker regarding why plaintiff disciplined. Also, Triable Issue of Fact of pretext because of disparate treatment. ORDER AND OJBECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS signed by the Court, copy given to counsel prior to the hearing. ORDER RE PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO EVIDENCE TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMMARY ADJUDICATION signed by the Court, copy given to counsel prior to the hearing. Off Calendar Apr-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
John Houston Scott, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Clifford Cook
Margaret W. Baumgartner, Deputy City Attorney, for Defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, Plaintiff Clifford Cook's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Jason Acosta, Jennifer Northrop, Sean McCalstrom, Kelly Paul, And Heather Fong is taken under submission. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Deposition Of Lt. Robert Moser
Off Calendar Apr-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Margaret W. Baumgartner, Deputy City Attorney, for defendant and moving party: City and County of San Francisco
John Houston Scott, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Clifford Cook

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion For Protective Order re: Deposition Of Lt. Robert Moser, granted, as follows:
a. Lt. Moser shall appear at deposition, and provide testimony limited to the matters set forth in any declarations of the deponent previously submitted in this action, including methods or procedures used (i) in gathering information to prepare responses to discovery request for which he provided verification, and (ii) the court-ordered chart of disciplined officers.
b. In connection with Lt. Moser's deposition, the deponent shall not disclose the identity, nor any information contained in the personnel file of any officer included in the chart. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Re-Notice Of Motion To Disclose Peace Officer Personnel Records, Or, Alternatively, To Disclose Records Under Evidence Code 1040
Off Calendar Nov-20-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
JOHN SCOTT, ESQ., (415)561-9601, FOR Plaintiff/MOVING PARTY, Clifford Cook
MARGARET W. BAUMGARTNER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, (415)554-3859, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ET. AL.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS WITH MODIFICATION. Plaintiff Clifford Cook's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted in part. As to the interrogatories, the Court will modify the questions to only require disclosure of suspensions pursuant to San Francisco Charter Section A8.341 for acts which would constitute a felony or misdemeanor involving moral turpitude. Furthermore, defendant may redact the names of said persons while assigning a numerical identifier. The responses shall identify the ethnicity of any officers. Defendant shall produce any responsive documents for in camera review. MODIFICATION: TENTATIVE MODIFIED TO REQUIRE DISCLOSURE OF WHETHER ANY OFFICERS SUSPENDED WERE FORMALLY CHARGED BY COMPLAINT OR INDICTMENT WITH COMMITTING A PUBLIC OFFENSE. DEFENDANT SHALL SUBMIT DOCUMENTS FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW IN 30 DAYS. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported. (610/BEC)

To Disclose Peace Officer Personnel Records

No appearance, Plaintiff's motion To Disclose Peace Officer Personnel Records, to be heard on 11/20/08, per amended notice. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
